map.noJournal: No journal entry for this ride.

about.title: About
about.goalText: Ride from Deadhorse (Alaska) to Ushuaia (Argentina) — roughly 30,000 km across two continents, from Arctic ice to Patagonian winds. A fully self-supported journey departing summer 2025, shaped by roads, mountain passes and encounters along the way.
about.vincentText: Vincent Morisetti is a Swiss cyclist with a passion for adventure and wide-open spaces. After years of riding in the Alps and dreaming of a grand journey, he planned everything to cross both Americas by bike — from the Arctic Circle to Tierra del Fuego. An athlete at heart and endlessly curious about the cultures he rides through, he documents the adventure on video and Instagram to share every stage with his community.
about.sponsorsText: This journey is made possible thanks to the support of our partners. Want to support the adventure? Get in touch.
about.guideIntro: This map follows Vincent's journey from Deadhorse, Alaska to Ushuaia, Argentina. Here is what you can see, where the data comes from and how to read it.
about.guideSectionMapRides: Orange traces — rides recorded by Vincent. Click a trace to open the detail panel: distance, elevation gain, maximum altitude, top speed, breaks longer than 10 minutes, and the ride journal if he wrote one. The title and description come from the Strava activity by default; Vincent can edit them manually but does not always have the time or energy to do so.
about.guideSectionMapRoute: Dashed cyan line — the planned itinerary from Deadhorse to Ushuaia. It serves as a reference to measure progress.
about.guideSectionMapPhotos: Photo markers — geotagged photos from Vincent's Strava activities and his Flickr account. Only photos with GPS coordinates appear on the map. Click to enlarge.
about.guideSectionMapWeather: Weather layer (dedicated button) — current conditions sampled roughly every 50 km along the planned route: temperature, humidity, wind direction and animated speed. This layer only appears from a zoom level equivalent to viewing a large country (around level 7). Data is refreshed every day at 06:00 UTC alongside all other data.
about.guideSectionMapCities: City markers (?) — major towns near the route, visible from a regional zoom level (level 9, roughly the size of a province). Relief markers — notable mountains, passes and lakes along the route, visible from a country-level zoom (level 7). Click any marker to read a Wikipedia summary in your language.
about.guideSectionMapBasemap: Base map — the button in the top-left corner lets you switch the background map (satellite, terrain, classic). The choice is not remembered between sessions.
about.guideSectionMapPosition: Pulsing cyan dot — Vincent's position on the map corresponds to the end point of his last recorded Strava activity.
about.guideSectionDataTitle: Where does the data come from?
about.guideSectionDataStrava: Rides and photos: imported automatically from Strava, Vincent's activity tracking app. He must publish activities manually from his phone; depending on field conditions (connectivity, fatigue, available time) there can be a lag of several days. Do not worry if the map does not move for a few days — it is completely normal.
about.guideSectionDataFlickr: Additional photos: from his Flickr account, for photos taken outside Strava with GPS coordinates.
about.guideSectionDataYoutube: Videos: imported from his YouTube channel.
about.guideSectionDataWeather: Weather: via the Open-Meteo API, refreshed every day at 06:00 UTC.
about.guideSectionDataWiki: City and point-of-interest descriptions: fetched on demand from Wikipedia, in your language when available, otherwise in English.
about.guideSectionFreshnessTitle: How up to date is the data?
about.guideSectionFreshnessText: All data (rides, photos, videos, weather) is synchronised once a day at 06:00 UTC. Vincent's progress depends on his ability to publish Strava activities from the field. Several days may pass without an update — this is normal and does not mean the journey has stopped.
about.guideSectionSuggestText: Have an idea to improve the map or the site? Send us a message.
